transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yeah.
What is your main medical concern today?
Yes.
Can you please describe the main problem or symptom that brought you to call today?
I have a problem with my eye.
What specific problem are you having with your eye?
I got laundry detergent in it.
How long ago did the laundry detergent get into your eye?
This morning.
What symptoms are you experiencing in the eye since the detergent exposure, such as pain, redness, vision changes, or discharge?
Burning.
Have you rinsed your eye with water or any other solution since the detergent got in it?
I have. I did.
Any changes in your vision or worsening pain since the exposure?
No.
Any redness, swelling, or discharge from the eye since the detergent exposure?
No.
Any difficulty opening the eye or sensitivity to light since the exposure?
No.
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
No.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Email.
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
No.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you got laundry detergent in your eye, which is likely causing chemical irritation. Since you don’t have redness, swelling, discharge, or vision changes, this is probably a mild chemical conjunctivitis. For now, you can use artificial tears to help soothe your eye. A licensed physician will review this note and may recommend medication if needed. If you notice any worsening symptoms like increased pain, redness, swelling, discharge, trouble opening your eye, or any vision changes, please seek in-person care the same day. If you develop severe eye pain or sudden vision loss, go to the emergency room right away. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or do tests, so if symptoms don’t improve in a day or two, consider getting an in-person evaluation.
